Key Finding: Active customers retain better than inactive customers.
Inactive customers contribute more churn than their customer share.
Metric Evidence: Inactive customers: 48.49% customer share but 63.92% churn contribution

Key Finding: High product load customers show extreme churn risk.
Metric Evidence: High Product Load + Inactive churn rate: 90.22%

Key Finding: Active customers with optimal product depth show the strongest retention.
Metric Evidence: Optimal Product Depth + Active churn rate: 5.56%

Key Finding: Germany inactive customers show the highest geography-engagement churn risk.
High-value disengaged customers show elevated churn risk.
Active customers have much stronger relationship strength than inactive customers.
